subject: Sociology
title: Karl Marx and Class Conflict
description: Learn Karl Marx and Class Conflict in one minute.

hook.text: A factory owner and a factory worker live in the same city but inhabit different worlds. Marx asked why.

buildup.text: Marx argued that society is shaped by economic relations. Those who own production control wealth and power.

discovery.text: He identified class conflict as history's engine: the bourgeoisie exploits the proletariat's labor for profit.

twist.text: Marx predicted revolution, but capitalism adapted. Welfare states and unions softened the conflict he expected.

climax.text: His core insight endures: economic inequality shapes politics, culture, and daily life in ways people rarely see.

punchline.text: Marx didn't predict the future. He exposed the present.

quiz.question: What did Marx see as the engine of historical change?
options: Class conflict between owners and workers
